item.title: Neuberger Space Fund Turns to Europe as SpaceX Hype Buoys Sector
item.description: Evelyn Chow’s space-focused stock fund is scouring Europe for bargains just as the looming debut of SpaceX supercharges valuations in the US.

item.title: Cities Need to Boost Green Investment for Safety, EIB Chief Says
item.description: Cities are increasing investment in the green transition to protect against the potentially catastrophic effects of climate change, the European Investment Bank president said.

item.title: Goldman, Nomura Cave on PBOC Stimulus After Politburo Restraint
item.description: Economists at Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and Nomura Holdings Inc. joined peers at other major global banks in abandoning calls for China’s monetary easing this year, as top leaders convey confidence in growth and little urgency to step up support.

item.title: Solaria Raises €300 Million to Fund Data Center, Battery Boost
item.description: Spanish clean-energy producer Solaria Energía y Medio Ambiente SA has raised about €300 million in a share offering, with the funds to be deployed in expanding its data center and battery storage operations.

item.title: Millennium, NBIM Are Said to Invest in CATL’s $5 Billion Share Sale
item.description: Hedge-fund firm Millennium Management and Norway’s sovereign-wealth fund Norges Bank Investment Management are among those that participated in battery maker Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd.’s $5 billion share sale, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Morocco Firm Plans First Fundraise for $25 Billion Gas Pipeline
item.description: Morocco’s state-controlled natural resources company plans to start raising part of the $25 billion needed to build a pipeline that will link gas fields in West Africa to the Mediterranean coastline.

item.title: Japan Confirms Hormuz Transit as Iran Talks Continue, PM Says
item.description: Japan’s prime minister confirmed that a ship linked to the country has transited the Strait of Hormuz, as Tokyo continues to lobby Tehran for the safe passage of all vessels through the chokepoint.

item.title: Crop Prices Hit Highest Since 2023 as War and Bad Weather Bite
item.description: The extended closure of the Strait of Hormuz and extreme weather have jolted the price index for farm commodities to a two-year high as fertilizer headaches and the prospect of smaller harvests drive food inflation risks.

item.title: European Airports Warn of Tough Outlook as War Disrupts Flights
item.description: Operators of Europe’s largest airports are warning of an increasingly uncertain outlook as the Middle East conflict adjusts flight schedules and restricts access to key airspaces.

item.title: Deutsche Bank Flags Commercial Property as Risk Afer Hit
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG suffered a dent in its exposure to commercial real estate and highlighted that the asset continues to be a problem for it.

item.title: Thailand Holds Key Rate to Support Economy Amid Oil Shock
item.description: The Bank of Thailand left its benchmark interest rate at a near four-year low, focusing for now on supporting the economy from the drag of the war in Iran rather than its potential inflationary impact.

item.title: Spanish Inflation Unexpectedly Quickens Further Beyond ECB Goal
item.description: Spanish inflation unexpectedly accelerated further beyond the European Central Bank’s 2% target, supporting market expectations that interest rates will be raised this year due to the Iran war.

item.title: EU Escalates Probe Into Meta for Failing to Stop Kids Signing Up
item.description: The European Union’s executive arm accused Meta Platforms Inc. of failing to keep young children off its services, ramping up a probe that could result in hefty fines.

item.title: UzNIF’s London IPO Set to Value Uzbekistan Fund at $1.95 Billion
item.description: Uzbekistan’s national investment fund’s initial public offering is set to value the vehicle at about $1.95 billion, as it offers investors a rare way to invest into Central Asia’s second-biggest economy.

item.title: UBS Boss Ermotti Warns Investors on Iran Over-Confidence
item.description: UBS Group AG Chief Executive Officer Sergio Ermotti said that financial markets are at risk of over-optimism regarding the Iran conflict.

item.title: Hong Kong Has Widest Trade Deficit Since 1952 in Echo of AI Boom
item.description: Hong Kong ran its widest trade deficit in at least 74 years as imports surged, a likely spillover from the disruptions caused by the war in the Middle East and a global investment boom in artificial intelligence that’s driving demand for tech products.

item.title: Haleon’s First-Quarter Sales Dragged Down by Weak Cold Season
item.description: Haleon Plc’s first quarter was weaker than expected, hurt by lackluster demand for over-the-counter cold and flu medicines at the start of the year.

item.title: Aston Martin Posts Another Loss as Carmaker’s Debt Swells
item.description: Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings Plc reported another quarterly loss as the embattled carmaker struggles to engineer a turnaround under billionaire Lawrence Stroll.

item.title: Carlsberg Sales Volumes Rise as Alcohol-Free Push Gains Traction
item.description: Carlsberg A/S started the year with positive sales volumes in all regions, a sign the Danish brewer’s strategy to diversify into alcohol-free drinks is working in spite of disruptions from the Iran war.

item.title: GSK Profit Beats Estimates on HIV, Cancer Medicines
item.description: GSK Plc’s profit was higher than analysts expected in the first quarter, helped by the drugmaker’s specialty medicines for HIV, cancer and immune diseases.

item.title: Founder Gets 9-Year Jail Term in $300 Million Indonesia Case
item.description: Indonesia handed eFishery founder Gibran Huzaifah a nine-year prison sentence on Wednesday, concluding a high-profile $300 million financial scandal that destroyed one of Southeast Asia’s most celebrated startups.

item.title: Amadeus Agrees to Buy Idemia Unit for €1.2 Billion
item.description: Amadeus IT SA said it has agreed to buy the public security arm of Advent-backed biometrics technology provider Idemia for €1.2 billion ($1.4 billion).

item.title: Central Banks ‘Scoop Up a Load’ of Gold in Bumpy First Quarter
item.description: Central banks added gold holdings at the fastest pace in more than a year in the first quarter, as a slump in prices encouraged a wave of buying that more than offset sales by a handful of institutions.

item.title: Distressed UK Firms Pile Up as War Enters Third Month, Report Says
item.description: UK companies faced a sharp jump in “critical” financial distress at the start of 2026, underscoring the mounting pressure that rising costs and weak consumer demand are placing on businesses as the war in the Middle East enters its third month.

item.title: Blackstone to Invest €2 Billion in Renewables Developer Eurowind
item.description: Blackstone Inc. has agreed to invest as much as €2 billion ($2.3 billion) in Eurowind Energy, a pan-European renewables developer and independent power producer.

item.title: Finland’s Kone Agrees to Buy Rival TK Elevator for €29.4 Billion
item.description: Kone Oyj agreed to acquire TK Elevator for €29.4 billion ($34.4 billion) including debt, in what will be one of Europe’s biggest-ever private equity exits.

item.title: Sri Lanka Discloses New Payment Lapse Days After Hack Revelation
item.description: Sri Lanka’s government has disclosed a payment lapse involving funds due to the US Post, days after revealing a separate cyberattack, raising fresh questions about its financial safeguards as it continues to recover from an unprecedented default.

item.title: James Murdoch-Backed Allen Career Institute Is Said to Consider India IPO
item.description: Allen Career Institute, an Indian education services company backed by James Murdoch and ex-Disney executive Uday Shankar, is considering an initial public offering in Mumbai, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: FTSE 100 Futures, Pound Fall as Lloyds and Astra Report Earnings
Deutsche Bank CFO Says Trading Business Started Well in April
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG saw its fixed income and currencies trading business start “constructively” in the second quarter after some jitters in March, Chief Financial Officer Raja Akram said in an interview.
